stonetop.summary: A house-sized badger-sloth that tears whole slopes of trees apart.

system.concept: A house-sized badger-sloth that tears whole slopes of trees apart.
system.description: Think of something like a badger or a sloth, but big as a house: twenty feet long, nine feet at the shoulder, forearms nearly twelve feet long when you include those shovel-like claws. Mostly they just lumber about looking for their favorite trees to tear up and chew through, sometimes clearing whole slopes. Just about everything gives them a wide berth — even apex predators like a kleztigr don't think they're worth the trouble. Sometimes, though, you round a bend and there one is, and you'd best circle around, quiet-like.
